= Mito Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right, nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}} 87 Terada Mitosaka |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami}}
{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami}}
{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=fusha|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} to early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}} |本殿 = One-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mito Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mito Shrine''' (みとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Terada Mitosaka, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=fusha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Located at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, it was the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}} of old {{ill|lt=Terada Village (Kyoto Prefecture)|Q30937244|WD=Q30937244|lt=Terada Village}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami}} (あまてらすすめおおみかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami}} (たかみむすびのかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} (わだつみとよたまひめのみこと) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, but because a fragment of ''Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki'' records "Mito no yashiro in Mizuwatari, Kuse District," it is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, when the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534}} were compiled. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} compiled in the early Heian period, it appears as "Mito Shrine, three seats." It was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), promoted to {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}} (Meiji 15), and promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=fusha|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} (Meiji 40). The rank system was later abolished; it now belongs to {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}} and is registered as "Religious Corporation Mito Shrine." === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Mito no Kami." === Precinct === The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, one bay front and two bays side. The roof is {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} with a large {{ill|lt=chidori hafu|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511}} gable on the front. At the center of the front porch are openwork {{ill|lt=karakusa pattern|Q732328|WD=Q732328|lt=arabesque}} carvings and {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911}} braces, making a simple yet elegant structure. A {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834}} indicates construction in {{ill|lt=1448|Q6439|WD=Q6439}} ({{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} 5) in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. It is the oldest surviving building in Joyo City and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}. File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Mito Jinja Kyoto Honden2.jpg|Honden (side and rear) File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haisho.jpg|Haisho File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haiden.jpg|Haiden === Festivals === === Annual grand festival === * Every year from {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881}} to {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932}} ** On September 30, the welcoming of the deity called "Oide" is performed in an orderly procession from the otabi site, centered on child mikoshi bearers, with strict order and role assignments for ritual implements. ** On the night of October 1, "Yomiya," a "yudate ritual" is held before the deity at the otabi site, in which a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139}} sprinkles hot water from an iron kettle with bamboo on worshipers to pray for safety. ** On October 2, "Matsuri," the deity is escorted back to the shrine in procession. === Okage dance === Mito Shrine preserves the Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property "votive ema of the Okage dance." The ema depicts an Okage dance dedicated on November 1, {{ill|lt=1830|Q7576|WD=Q7576}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 13) by people of Kita-higashi-machi, Terada Village. It shows that "Okage dance," said to have often followed group pilgrimages ("Okage-mairi") to Ise Jingu during the Edo period, was also performed here. In Joyo, dances seem to have been performed at festivals in Terada and nearby Aodani until the early Showa period. Today, the local "Okage Dance Preservation Society" has revived and continues the tradition, offering the dance at Mito Shrine.Joyo City Cultural Arts Promotion Plan (March 2007, Joyo City Board of Education Office), [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/government/plan/plan_culture/download/plan_culture.pdf ''PDF, related pages include pp.25, 27, 50, 51, 54''] === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Mito Shrine honden (with 8 munafuda boards) (building) - built in 1448 (Bun'an 5), mid-Muromachi period. Designated April 14, 1906 (Meiji 39){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1987|Mito Shrine Honden}}. === Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural properties === * Tangible folk cultural property ** Ema painting of Okage dance - late Edo period work. Registered March 19, 1999 (Heisei 11). === Joyo City designated cultural properties === [[ファイル:Daihannyakyo.jpg|サムネイル|Daihannya Sutra]] * Tangible cultural properties ** Iron kettle - thought to have been used in yudate rituals; inscription shows it was made in {{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|WD=Q6283}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 32). ** Complete set of 601 volumes of Daihannya Sutra and 7 sutra chests - sutra copies dating to early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, important materials for understanding village faith history. Deposited at Joyo City History and Folklore Museum. === Approach === [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), otorii.jpg|thumb|First torii facing old Yamato Kaido, rebuilt September 1966]] [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), torii.jpg|thumb|Second torii at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama; honden is up the stairs ahead]] About 200 m south of JR Joyo Station stands the "first torii" facing the old Yamato Kaido. From there, passing eastward through the "second torii" at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, the honden is about 600 m ahead. The approach is a gentle uphill slope toward the shrine. It is said that teahouses once lined this approach and that it bustled with worshipers. Though this approach lies roughly in the city center, both sides are lined with old pines, and the shrine surroundings are shi-oak woodland with red pine, preserving rich natural environment amid urbanization. Mito Shrine and its pine-lined approach were selected among the "200 Natural Sites of Kyoto."Kyoto Prefecture website, [http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical36.html ''Mito Shrine and the pine-lined approach''] === Access === * 1 km east from {{ill|lt=Joyo Station|Q1049427|WD=Q1049427}} on the {{ill|lt=Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}, north side of {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefectural Joyo High School|Q11375241|WD=Q11375241}}. * Immediately at "Joyo Koko" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Keihan Bus|Q11375841|WD=Q11375841}} "Joyo San-san Bus". === Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=373&r=1520450646.0538 Mito Shrine] - {{ill|lt=Kyoto Tourism Federation|Q101072265|WD=Q101072265}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Mito Shrine (Q11548558)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}} to early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}} |本殿 = One-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mito Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mito Shrine''' (みとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Terada Mitosaka, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}}. Located at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, it was the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097}} of old {{ill|lt=Terada Village (Kyoto Prefecture)|Q30937244|WD=Q30937244|lt=Terada Village}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami}} (あまてらすすめおおみかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami}} (たかみむすびのかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto}} (わだつみとよたまひめのみこと) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}, but because a fragment of ''Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki'' records "Mito no yashiro in Mizuwatari, Kuse District," it is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178}}, when the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534}} were compiled. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} compiled in the early Heian period, it appears as "Mito Shrine, three seats." It was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), promoted to {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}} (Meiji 15), and promoted to {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}} in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} (Meiji 40). The rank system was later abolished; it now belongs to {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077}} and is registered as "Religious Corporation Mito Shrine." === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Mito no Kami." === Precinct === The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}, one bay front and two bays side. The roof is {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} with a large {{ill|lt=chidori hafu|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511}} gable on the front. At the center of the front porch are openwork {{ill|lt=karakusa pattern|Q732328|WD=Q732328|lt=arabesque}} carvings and {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911}} braces, making a simple yet elegant structure. A {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834}} indicates construction in {{ill|lt=1448|Q6439|WD=Q6439}} ({{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} 5) in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. It is the oldest surviving building in Joyo City and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}. File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Mito Jinja Kyoto Honden2.jpg|Honden (side and rear) File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haisho.jpg|Haisho File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haiden.jpg|Haiden === Festivals === === Annual grand festival === * Every year from {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881}} to {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932}} ** On September 30, the welcoming of the deity called "Oide" is performed in an orderly procession from the otabi site, centered on child mikoshi bearers, with strict order and role assignments for ritual implements. ** On the night of October 1, "Yomiya," a "yudate ritual" is held before the deity at the otabi site, in which a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139}} sprinkles hot water from an iron kettle with bamboo on worshipers to pray for safety. ** On October 2, "Matsuri," the deity is escorted back to the shrine in procession. === Okage dance === Mito Shrine preserves the Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property "votive ema of the Okage dance." The ema depicts an Okage dance dedicated on November 1, {{ill|lt=1830|Q7576|WD=Q7576}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 13) by people of Kita-higashi-machi, Terada Village. It shows that "Okage dance," said to have often followed group pilgrimages ("Okage-mairi") to Ise Jingu during the Edo period, was also performed here. In Joyo, dances seem to have been performed at festivals in Terada and nearby Aodani until the early Showa period. Today, the local "Okage Dance Preservation Society" has revived and continues the tradition, offering the dance at Mito Shrine.Joyo City Cultural Arts Promotion Plan (March 2007, Joyo City Board of Education Office), [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/government/plan/plan_culture/download/plan_culture.pdf ''PDF, related pages include pp.25, 27, 50, 51, 54''] === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Mito Shrine honden (with 8 munafuda boards) (building) - built in 1448 (Bun'an 5), mid-Muromachi period. Designated April 14, 1906 (Meiji 39){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1987|Mito Shrine Honden}}. === Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural properties === * Tangible folk cultural property ** Ema painting of Okage dance - late Edo period work. Registered March 19, 1999 (Heisei 11). === Joyo City designated cultural properties === [[ファイル:Daihannyakyo.jpg|サムネイル|Daihannya Sutra]] * Tangible cultural properties ** Iron kettle - thought to have been used in yudate rituals; inscription shows it was made in {{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|WD=Q6283}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 32). ** Complete set of 601 volumes of Daihannya Sutra and 7 sutra chests - sutra copies dating to early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, important materials for understanding village faith history. Deposited at Joyo City History and Folklore Museum. === Approach === [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), otorii.jpg|thumb|First torii facing old Yamato Kaido, rebuilt September 1966]] [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), torii.jpg|thumb|Second torii at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama; honden is up the stairs ahead]] About 200 m south of JR Joyo Station stands the "first torii" facing the old Yamato Kaido. From there, passing eastward through the "second torii" at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, the honden is about 600 m ahead. The approach is a gentle uphill slope toward the shrine. It is said that teahouses once lined this approach and that it bustled with worshipers. Though this approach lies roughly in the city center, both sides are lined with old pines, and the shrine surroundings are shi-oak woodland with red pine, preserving rich natural environment amid urbanization. Mito Shrine and its pine-lined approach were selected among the "200 Natural Sites of Kyoto."Kyoto Prefecture website, [http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical36.html ''Mito Shrine and the pine-lined approach''] === Access === * 1 km east from {{ill|lt=Joyo Station|Q1049427|WD=Q1049427}} on the {{ill|lt=Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}, north side of {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefectural Joyo High School|Q11375241|WD=Q11375241}}. * Immediately at "Joyo Koko" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Keihan Bus|Q11375841|WD=Q11375841}} "Joyo San-san Bus". === Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=373&r=1520450646.0538 Mito Shrine] - {{ill|lt=Kyoto Tourism Federation|Q101072265|WD=Q101072265}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Mito Shrine (Q11548558)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It shows that "Okage dance," said to have often followed group pilgrimages ("Okage-mairi") to Ise Jingu during the Edo period, was also performed here. In Joyo, dances seem to have been performed at festivals in Terada and nearby Aodani until the early Showa period. Today, the local "Okage Dance Preservation Society" has revived and continues the tradition, offering the dance at Mito Shrine.Joyo City Cultural Arts Promotion Plan (March 2007, Joyo City Board of Education Office), [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/government/plan/plan_culture/download/plan_culture.pdf ''PDF, related pages include pp.25, 27, 50, 51, 54''] === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Mito Shrine honden (with 8 munafuda boards) (building) - built in 1448 (Bun'an 5), mid-Muromachi period. Designated April 14, 1906 (Meiji 39){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1987|Mito Shrine Honden}}. === Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural properties === * Tangible folk cultural property ** Ema painting of Okage dance - late Edo period work. Registered March 19, 1999 (Heisei 11). === Joyo City designated cultural properties === [[ファイル:Daihannyakyo.jpg|サムネイル|Daihannya Sutra]] * Tangible cultural properties ** Iron kettle - thought to have been used in yudate rituals; inscription shows it was made in {{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|WD=Q6283}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078}} 32). ** Complete set of 601 volumes of Daihannya Sutra and 7 sutra chests - sutra copies dating to early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, important materials for understanding village faith history. Deposited at Joyo City History and Folklore Museum. === Approach === [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), otorii.jpg|thumb|First torii facing old Yamato Kaido, rebuilt September 1966]] [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), torii.jpg|thumb|Second torii at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama; honden is up the stairs ahead]] About 200 m south of JR Joyo Station stands the "first torii" facing the old Yamato Kaido. From there, passing eastward through the "second torii" at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, the honden is about 600 m ahead. The approach is a gentle uphill slope toward the shrine. It is said that teahouses once lined this approach and that it bustled with worshipers. Though this approach lies roughly in the city center, both sides are lined with old pines, and the shrine surroundings are shi-oak woodland with red pine, preserving rich natural environment amid urbanization. Mito Shrine and its pine-lined approach were selected among the "200 Natural Sites of Kyoto."Kyoto Prefecture website, [http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical36.html ''Mito Shrine and the pine-lined approach''] === Access === * 1 km east from {{ill|lt=Joyo Station|Q1049427|WD=Q1049427}} on the {{ill|lt=Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}}, north side of {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefectural Joyo High School|Q11375241|WD=Q11375241}}. * Immediately at "Joyo Koko" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Keihan Bus|Q11375841|WD=Q11375841}} "Joyo San-san Bus". === Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=373&r=1520450646.0538 Mito Shrine] - {{ill|lt=Kyoto Tourism Federation|Q101072265|WD=Q101072265}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Mito Shrine (Q11548558)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Mito-Jinja, Joyo}} [[JA:水度神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11548558}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mito Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right, nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}} 87 Terada Mitosaka |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}} to early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} |本殿 = One-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mito Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mito Shrine''' (みとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Terada Mitosaka, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}}. Located at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, it was the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} of old {{ill|lt=Terada Village (Kyoto Prefecture)|Q30937244|WD=Q30937244|lt=Terada Village|1=Terada}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすすめおおみかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (わだつみとよたまひめのみこと) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, but because a fragment of ''Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki'' records "Mito no yashiro in Mizuwatari, Kuse District," it is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, when the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}} were compiled. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} compiled in the early Heian period, it appears as "Mito Shrine, three seats." It was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), promoted to {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15), and promoted to {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40). The rank system was later abolished; it now belongs to {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}} and is registered as "Religious Corporation Mito Shrine." === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Mito no Kami." === Precinct === The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, one bay front and two bays side. The roof is {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} with a large {{ill|lt=chidori hafu|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}} gable on the front. At the center of the front porch are openwork {{ill|lt=karakusa pattern|Q732328|WD=Q732328|lt=arabesque|1=Scroll (art)}} carvings and {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} braces, making a simple yet elegant structure. A {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} indicates construction in {{ill|lt=1448|Q6439|WD=Q6439|1=1448}} ({{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 5) in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is the oldest surviving building in Joyo City and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Mito Jinja Kyoto Honden2.jpg|Honden (side and rear) File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haisho.jpg|Haisho File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haiden.jpg|Haiden === Festivals === === Annual grand festival === * Every year from {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} to {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932|1=October 2}} ** On September 30, the welcoming of the deity called "Oide" is performed in an orderly procession from the otabi site, centered on child mikoshi bearers, with strict order and role assignments for ritual implements. ** On the night of October 1, "Yomiya," a "yudate ritual" is held before the deity at the otabi site, in which a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}} sprinkles hot water from an iron kettle with bamboo on worshipers to pray for safety. ** On October 2, "Matsuri," the deity is escorted back to the shrine in procession. === Okage dance === Mito Shrine preserves the Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property "votive ema of the Okage dance." The ema depicts an Okage dance dedicated on November 1, {{ill|lt=1830|Q7576|WD=Q7576|1=1830}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 13) by people of Kita-higashi-machi, Terada Village. It shows that "Okage dance," said to have often followed group pilgrimages ("Okage-mairi") to Ise Jingu during the Edo period, was also performed here. In Joyo, dances seem to have been performed at festivals in Terada and nearby Aodani until the early Showa period. Today, the local "Okage Dance Preservation Society" has revived and continues the tradition, offering the dance at Mito Shrine.Joyo City Cultural Arts Promotion Plan (March 2007, Joyo City Board of Education Office), [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/government/plan/plan_culture/download/plan_culture.pdf ''PDF, related pages include pp.25, 27, 50, 51, 54''] === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Mito Shrine honden (with 8 munafuda boards) (building) - built in 1448 (Bun'an 5), mid-Muromachi period. Designated April 14, 1906 (Meiji 39){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1987|Mito Shrine Honden}}. === Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural properties === * Tangible folk cultural property ** Ema painting of Okage dance - late Edo period work. Registered March 19, 1999 (Heisei 11). === Joyo City designated cultural properties === [[ファイル:Daihannyakyo.jpg|サムネイル|Daihannya Sutra]] * Tangible cultural properties ** Iron kettle - thought to have been used in yudate rituals; inscription shows it was made in {{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|WD=Q6283|1=1425}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 32). ** Complete set of 601 volumes of Daihannya Sutra and 7 sutra chests - sutra copies dating to early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, important materials for understanding village faith history. Deposited at Joyo City History and Folklore Museum. === Approach === [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), otorii.jpg|thumb|First torii facing old Yamato Kaido, rebuilt September 1966]] [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), torii.jpg|thumb|Second torii at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama; honden is up the stairs ahead]] About 200 m south of JR Joyo Station stands the "first torii" facing the old Yamato Kaido. From there, passing eastward through the "second torii" at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, the honden is about 600 m ahead. The approach is a gentle uphill slope toward the shrine. It is said that teahouses once lined this approach and that it bustled with worshipers. Though this approach lies roughly in the city center, both sides are lined with old pines, and the shrine surroundings are shi-oak woodland with red pine, preserving rich natural environment amid urbanization. Mito Shrine and its pine-lined approach were selected among the "200 Natural Sites of Kyoto."Kyoto Prefecture website, [http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical36.html ''Mito Shrine and the pine-lined approach''] === Access === * 1 km east from {{ill|lt=Joyo Station|Q1049427|WD=Q1049427|1=Jōyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}, north side of {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefectural Joyo High School|Q11375241|WD=Q11375241|1=Jyouyou High School}}. * Immediately at "Joyo Koko" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Keihan Bus|Q11375841|WD=Q11375841|1=Kyoto Keihan Bus}} "Joyo San-san Bus". === Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=373&r=1520450646.0538 Mito Shrine] - {{ill|lt=Kyoto Tourism Federation|Q101072265|WD=Q101072265|1=Kyoto Tourism Federation}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Mito Shrine (Q11548558)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Mito-Jinja, Joyo}} [[JA:水度神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11548558}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mito Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right, nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}} 87 Terada Mitosaka |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}} to early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} |本殿 = One-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mito Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mito Shrine''' (みとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Terada Mitosaka, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}}. Located at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, it was the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} of old {{ill|lt=Terada Village (Kyoto Prefecture)|Q30937244|WD=Q30937244|lt=Terada Village|1=Terada}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすすめおおみかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (わだつみとよたまひめのみこと) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, but because a fragment of ''Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki'' records "Mito no yashiro in Mizuwatari, Kuse District," it is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, when the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}} were compiled. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} compiled in the early Heian period, it appears as "Mito Shrine, three seats." It was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), promoted to {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15), and promoted to {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40). The rank system was later abolished; it now belongs to {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}} and is registered as "Religious Corporation Mito Shrine." === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Mito no Kami." === Precinct === The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, one bay front and two bays side. The roof is {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} with a large {{ill|lt=chidori hafu|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}} gable on the front. At the center of the front porch are openwork {{ill|lt=karakusa pattern|Q732328|WD=Q732328|lt=arabesque|1=Scroll (art)}} carvings and {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} braces, making a simple yet elegant structure. A {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} indicates construction in {{ill|lt=1448|Q6439|WD=Q6439|1=1448}} ({{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 5) in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is the oldest surviving building in Joyo City and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Mito Jinja Kyoto Honden2.jpg|Honden (side and rear) File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haisho.jpg|Haisho File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haiden.jpg|Haiden === Festivals === === Annual grand festival === * Every year from {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} to {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932|1=October 2}} ** On September 30, the welcoming of the deity called "Oide" is performed in an orderly procession from the otabi site, centered on child mikoshi bearers, with strict order and role assignments for ritual implements. ** On the night of October 1, "Yomiya," a "yudate ritual" is held before the deity at the otabi site, in which a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}} sprinkles hot water from an iron kettle with bamboo on worshipers to pray for safety. ** On October 2, "Matsuri," the deity is escorted back to the shrine in procession. === Okage dance === Mito Shrine preserves the Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property "votive ema of the Okage dance." The ema depicts an Okage dance dedicated on November 1, {{ill|lt=1830|Q7576|WD=Q7576|1=1830}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 13) by people of Kita-higashi-machi, Terada Village. It shows that "Okage dance," said to have often followed group pilgrimages ("Okage-mairi") to Ise Jingu during the Edo period, was also performed here. In Joyo, dances seem to have been performed at festivals in Terada and nearby Aodani until the early Showa period. Today, the local "Okage Dance Preservation Society" has revived and continues the tradition, offering the dance at Mito Shrine.Joyo City Cultural Arts Promotion Plan (March 2007, Joyo City Board of Education Office), [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/government/plan/plan_culture/download/plan_culture.pdf ''PDF, related pages include pp.25, 27, 50, 51, 54''] === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Mito Shrine honden (with 8 munafuda boards) (building) - built in 1448 (Bun'an 5), mid-Muromachi period. Designated April 14, 1906 (Meiji 39){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1987|Mito Shrine Honden}}. === Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural properties === * Tangible folk cultural property ** Ema painting of Okage dance - late Edo period work. Registered March 19, 1999 (Heisei 11). === Joyo City designated cultural properties === [[ファイル:Daihannyakyo.jpg|サムネイル|Daihannya Sutra]] * Tangible cultural properties ** Iron kettle - thought to have been used in yudate rituals; inscription shows it was made in {{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|WD=Q6283|1=1425}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 32). ** Complete set of 601 volumes of Daihannya Sutra and 7 sutra chests - sutra copies dating to early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, important materials for understanding village faith history. Deposited at Joyo City History and Folklore Museum. === Approach === [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), otorii.jpg|thumb|First torii facing old Yamato Kaido, rebuilt September 1966]] [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), torii.jpg|thumb|Second torii at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama; honden is up the stairs ahead]] About 200 m south of JR Joyo Station stands the "first torii" facing the old Yamato Kaido. From there, passing eastward through the "second torii" at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, the honden is about 600 m ahead. The approach is a gentle uphill slope toward the shrine. It is said that teahouses once lined this approach and that it bustled with worshipers. Though this approach lies roughly in the city center, both sides are lined with old pines, and the shrine surroundings are shi-oak woodland with red pine, preserving rich natural environment amid urbanization. Mito Shrine and its pine-lined approach were selected among the "200 Natural Sites of Kyoto."Kyoto Prefecture website, [http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical36.html ''Mito Shrine and the pine-lined approach''] === Access === * 1 km east from {{ill|lt=Joyo Station|Q1049427|WD=Q1049427|1=Jōyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}, north side of {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefectural Joyo High School|Q11375241|WD=Q11375241|1=Jyouyou High School}}. * Immediately at "Joyo Koko" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Keihan Bus|Q11375841|WD=Q11375841|1=Kyoto Keihan Bus}} "Joyo San-san Bus". === Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=373&r=1520450646.0538 Mito Shrine] - {{ill|lt=Kyoto Tourism Federation|Q101072265|WD=Q101072265|1=Kyoto Tourism Federation}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Mito Shrine (Q11548558)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Mito-Jinja, Joyo}} [[JA:水度神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11548558}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mito Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right, nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}} 87 Terada Mitosaka |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}} to early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} |本殿 = One-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mito Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mito Shrine''' (みとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Terada Mitosaka, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}}. Located at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, it was the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} of old {{ill|lt=Terada Village (Kyoto Prefecture)|Q30937244|WD=Q30937244|lt=Terada Village|1=Terada}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすすめおおみかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (わだつみとよたまひめのみこと) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, but because a fragment of ''Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki'' records "Mito no yashiro in Mizuwatari, Kuse District," it is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, when the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}} were compiled. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} compiled in the early Heian period, it appears as "Mito Shrine, three seats." It was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), promoted to {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15), and promoted to {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40). The rank system was later abolished; it now belongs to {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}} and is registered as "Religious Corporation Mito Shrine." === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Mito no Kami." === Precinct === The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, one bay front and two bays side. The roof is {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} with a large {{ill|lt=chidori hafu|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}} gable on the front. At the center of the front porch are openwork {{ill|lt=karakusa pattern|Q732328|WD=Q732328|lt=arabesque|1=Scroll (art)}} carvings and {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} braces, making a simple yet elegant structure. A {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} indicates construction in {{ill|lt=1448|Q6439|WD=Q6439|1=1448}} ({{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 5) in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is the oldest surviving building in Joyo City and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Mito Jinja Kyoto Honden2.jpg|Honden (side and rear) File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haisho.jpg|Haisho File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haiden.jpg|Haiden === Festivals === === Annual grand festival === * Every year from {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} to {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932|1=October 2}} ** On September 30, the welcoming of the deity called "Oide" is performed in an orderly procession from the otabi site, centered on child mikoshi bearers, with strict order and role assignments for ritual implements. ** On the night of October 1, "Yomiya," a "yudate ritual" is held before the deity at the otabi site, in which a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}} sprinkles hot water from an iron kettle with bamboo on worshipers to pray for safety. ** On October 2, "Matsuri," the deity is escorted back to the shrine in procession. === Okage dance === Mito Shrine preserves the Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property "votive ema of the Okage dance." The ema depicts an Okage dance dedicated on November 1, {{ill|lt=1830|Q7576|WD=Q7576|1=1830}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 13) by people of Kita-higashi-machi, Terada Village. It shows that "Okage dance," said to have often followed group pilgrimages ("Okage-mairi") to Ise Jingu during the Edo period, was also performed here. In Joyo, dances seem to have been performed at festivals in Terada and nearby Aodani until the early Showa period. Today, the local "Okage Dance Preservation Society" has revived and continues the tradition, offering the dance at Mito Shrine.Joyo City Cultural Arts Promotion Plan (March 2007, Joyo City Board of Education Office), [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/government/plan/plan_culture/download/plan_culture.pdf ''PDF, related pages include pp.25, 27, 50, 51, 54''] === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Mito Shrine honden (with 8 munafuda boards) (building) - built in 1448 (Bun'an 5), mid-Muromachi period. Designated April 14, 1906 (Meiji 39){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1987|Mito Shrine Honden}}. === Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural properties === * Tangible folk cultural property ** Ema painting of Okage dance - late Edo period work. Registered March 19, 1999 (Heisei 11). === Joyo City designated cultural properties === [[ファイル:Daihannyakyo.jpg|サムネイル|Daihannya Sutra]] * Tangible cultural properties ** Iron kettle - thought to have been used in yudate rituals; inscription shows it was made in {{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|WD=Q6283|1=1425}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 32). ** Complete set of 601 volumes of Daihannya Sutra and 7 sutra chests - sutra copies dating to early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, important materials for understanding village faith history. Deposited at Joyo City History and Folklore Museum. === Approach === [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), otorii.jpg|thumb|First torii facing old Yamato Kaido, rebuilt September 1966]] [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), torii.jpg|thumb|Second torii at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama; honden is up the stairs ahead]] About 200 m south of JR Joyo Station stands the "first torii" facing the old Yamato Kaido. From there, passing eastward through the "second torii" at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, the honden is about 600 m ahead. The approach is a gentle uphill slope toward the shrine. It is said that teahouses once lined this approach and that it bustled with worshipers. Though this approach lies roughly in the city center, both sides are lined with old pines, and the shrine surroundings are shi-oak woodland with red pine, preserving rich natural environment amid urbanization. Mito Shrine and its pine-lined approach were selected among the "200 Natural Sites of Kyoto."Kyoto Prefecture website, [http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical36.html ''Mito Shrine and the pine-lined approach''] === Access === * 1 km east from {{ill|lt=Joyo Station|Q1049427|WD=Q1049427|1=Jōyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}, north side of {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefectural Joyo High School|Q11375241|WD=Q11375241|1=Jyouyou High School}}. * Immediately at "Joyo Koko" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Keihan Bus|Q11375841|WD=Q11375841|1=Kyoto Keihan Bus}} "Joyo San-san Bus". === Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=373&r=1520450646.0538 Mito Shrine] - {{ill|lt=Kyoto Tourism Federation|Q101072265|WD=Q101072265|1=Kyoto Tourism Federation}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Mito Shrine (Q11548558)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Mito-Jinja, Joyo}} [[JA:水度神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11548558}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Mito Shrine |画像 = [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), keidai.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (left) and honden (rear right, nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}} 87 Terada Mitosaka |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}} to early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} |本殿 = One-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mito Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mito Shrine''' (みとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Terada Mitosaka, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}}. Located at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, it was the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} of old {{ill|lt=Terada Village (Kyoto Prefecture)|Q30937244|WD=Q30937244|lt=Terada Village|1=Terada}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすすめおおみかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (わだつみとよたまひめのみこと) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, but because a fragment of ''Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki'' records "Mito no yashiro in Mizuwatari, Kuse District," it is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, when the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}} were compiled. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} compiled in the early Heian period, it appears as "Mito Shrine, three seats." It was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), promoted to {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15), and promoted to {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40). The rank system was later abolished; it now belongs to {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}} and is registered as "Religious Corporation Mito Shrine." === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Mito no Kami." === Precinct === The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, one bay front and two bays side. The roof is {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} with a large {{ill|lt=chidori hafu|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}} gable on the front. At the center of the front porch are openwork {{ill|lt=karakusa pattern|Q732328|WD=Q732328|lt=arabesque|1=Scroll (art)}} carvings and {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} braces, making a simple yet elegant structure. A {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} indicates construction in {{ill|lt=1448|Q6439|WD=Q6439|1=1448}} ({{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 5) in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is the oldest surviving building in Joyo City and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Mito Jinja Kyoto Honden2.jpg|Honden (side and rear) File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haisho.jpg|Haisho File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haiden.jpg|Haiden === Festivals === === Annual grand festival === * Every year from {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} to {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932|1=October 2}} ** On September 30, the welcoming of the deity called "Oide" is performed in an orderly procession from the otabi site, centered on child mikoshi bearers, with strict order and role assignments for ritual implements. ** On the night of October 1, "Yomiya," a "yudate ritual" is held before the deity at the otabi site, in which a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}} sprinkles hot water from an iron kettle with bamboo on worshipers to pray for safety. ** On October 2, "Matsuri," the deity is escorted back to the shrine in procession. === Okage dance === Mito Shrine preserves the Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property "votive ema of the Okage dance." The ema depicts an Okage dance dedicated on November 1, {{ill|lt=1830|Q7576|WD=Q7576|1=1830}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 13) by people of Kita-higashi-machi, Terada Village. It shows that "Okage dance," said to have often followed group pilgrimages ("Okage-mairi") to Ise Jingu during the Edo period, was also performed here. In Joyo, dances seem to have been performed at festivals in Terada and nearby Aodani until the early Showa period. Today, the local "Okage Dance Preservation Society" has revived and continues the tradition, offering the dance at Mito Shrine.Joyo City Cultural Arts Promotion Plan (March 2007, Joyo City Board of Education Office), [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/government/plan/plan_culture/download/plan_culture.pdf ''PDF, related pages include pp.25, 27, 50, 51, 54''] === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Mito Shrine honden (with 8 munafuda boards) (building) - built in 1448 (Bun'an 5), mid-Muromachi period. Designated April 14, 1906 (Meiji 39){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1987|Mito Shrine Honden}}. === Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural properties === * Tangible folk cultural property ** Ema painting of Okage dance - late Edo period work. Registered March 19, 1999 (Heisei 11). === Joyo City designated cultural properties === [[ファイル:Daihannyakyo.jpg|サムネイル|Daihannya Sutra]] * Tangible cultural properties ** Iron kettle - thought to have been used in yudate rituals; inscription shows it was made in {{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|WD=Q6283|1=1425}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 32). ** Complete set of 601 volumes of Daihannya Sutra and 7 sutra chests - sutra copies dating to early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, important materials for understanding village faith history. Deposited at Joyo City History and Folklore Museum. === Approach === [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), otorii.jpg|thumb|First torii facing old Yamato Kaido, rebuilt September 1966]] [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), torii.jpg|thumb|Second torii at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama; honden is up the stairs ahead]] About 200 m south of JR Joyo Station stands the "first torii" facing the old Yamato Kaido. From there, passing eastward through the "second torii" at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, the honden is about 600 m ahead. The approach is a gentle uphill slope toward the shrine. It is said that teahouses once lined this approach and that it bustled with worshipers. Though this approach lies roughly in the city center, both sides are lined with old pines, and the shrine surroundings are shi-oak woodland with red pine, preserving rich natural environment amid urbanization. Mito Shrine and its pine-lined approach were selected among the "200 Natural Sites of Kyoto."Kyoto Prefecture website, [http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical36.html ''Mito Shrine and the pine-lined approach''] === Access === * 1 km east from {{ill|lt=Joyo Station|Q1049427|WD=Q1049427|1=Jōyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}, north side of {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefectural Joyo High School|Q11375241|WD=Q11375241|1=Jyouyou High School}}. * Immediately at "Joyo Koko" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Keihan Bus|Q11375841|WD=Q11375841|1=Kyoto Keihan Bus}} "Joyo San-san Bus". === Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=373&r=1520450646.0538 Mito Shrine] - {{ill|lt=Kyoto Tourism Federation|Q101072265|WD=Q101072265|1=Kyoto Tourism Federation}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みとしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QMito Shrine (Q11548558)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Mito Shrine (Q11548558)}}[[Category:qq]]

former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}} to early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} |本殿 = One-bay shrine {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Mito Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mito Shrine''' (みとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Terada Mitosaka, {{ill|lt=Joyo|Q817276|WD=Q817276|1=Jōyō, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}}. Located at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, it was the {{ill|lt=ubusunagami|Q1392097|WD=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} of old {{ill|lt=Terada Village (Kyoto Prefecture)|Q30937244|WD=Q30937244|lt=Terada Village|1=Terada}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Sumemioya Ōkami|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすすめおおみかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-kami|1=Takamimusubi}} (たかみむすびのかみ) * {{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Wadatsumi Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} (わだつみとよたまひめのみこと) === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded in the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, but because a fragment of ''Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki'' records "Mito no yashiro in Mizuwatari, Kuse District," it is thought to have already existed in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}, when the {{ill|lt=Fudoki|Q1194534|WD=Q1194534|1=Fudoki}} were compiled. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} compiled in the early Heian period, it appears as "Mito Shrine, three seats." It was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), promoted to {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15), and promoted to {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40). The rank system was later abolished; it now belongs to {{ill|lt=Jinja Honcho|Q908077|WD=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}} and is registered as "Religious Corporation Mito Shrine." === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Mito no Kami." === Precinct === The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, one bay front and two bays side. The roof is {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} with a large {{ill|lt=chidori hafu|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}} gable on the front. At the center of the front porch are openwork {{ill|lt=karakusa pattern|Q732328|WD=Q732328|lt=arabesque|1=Scroll (art)}} carvings and {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} braces, making a simple yet elegant structure. A {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} indicates construction in {{ill|lt=1448|Q6439|WD=Q6439|1=1448}} ({{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 5) in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is the oldest surviving building in Joyo City and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Mito Jinja Kyoto Honden2.jpg|Honden (side and rear) File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haisho.jpg|Haisho File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haiden.jpg|Haiden === Festivals === === Annual grand festival === * Every year from {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} to {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932|1=October 2}} ** On September 30, the welcoming of the deity called "Oide" is performed in an orderly procession from the otabi site, centered on child mikoshi bearers, with strict order and role assignments for ritual implements. ** On the night of October 1, "Yomiya," a "yudate ritual" is held before the deity at the otabi site, in which a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}} sprinkles hot water from an iron kettle with bamboo on worshipers to pray for safety. ** On October 2, "Matsuri," the deity is escorted back to the shrine in procession. === Okage dance === Mito Shrine preserves the Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property "votive ema of the Okage dance." The ema depicts an Okage dance dedicated on November 1, {{ill|lt=1830|Q7576|WD=Q7576|1=1830}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 13) by people of Kita-higashi-machi, Terada Village. It shows that "Okage dance," said to have often followed group pilgrimages ("Okage-mairi") to Ise Jingu during the Edo period, was also performed here. In Joyo, dances seem to have been performed at festivals in Terada and nearby Aodani until the early Showa period. Today, the local "Okage Dance Preservation Society" has revived and continues the tradition, offering the dance at Mito Shrine.Joyo City Cultural Arts Promotion Plan (March 2007, Joyo City Board of Education Office), [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/government/plan/plan_culture/download/plan_culture.pdf ''PDF, related pages include pp.25, 27, 50, 51, 54''] === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Mito Shrine honden (with 8 munafuda boards) (building) - built in 1448 (Bun'an 5), mid-Muromachi period. Designated April 14, 1906 (Meiji 39){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1987|Mito Shrine Honden}}. === Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural properties === * Tangible folk cultural property ** Ema painting of Okage dance - late Edo period work. Registered March 19, 1999 (Heisei 11). === Joyo City designated cultural properties === [[ファイル:Daihannyakyo.jpg|サムネイル|Daihannya Sutra]] * Tangible cultural properties ** Iron kettle - thought to have been used in yudate rituals; inscription shows it was made in {{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|WD=Q6283|1=1425}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 32). ** Complete set of 601 volumes of Daihannya Sutra and 7 sutra chests - sutra copies dating to early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, important materials for understanding village faith history. Deposited at Joyo City History and Folklore Museum. === Approach === [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), otorii.jpg|thumb|First torii facing old Yamato Kaido, rebuilt September 1966]] [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), torii.jpg|thumb|Second torii at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama; honden is up the stairs ahead]] About 200 m south of JR Joyo Station stands the "first torii" facing the old Yamato Kaido. From there, passing eastward through the "second torii" at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, the honden is about 600 m ahead. The approach is a gentle uphill slope toward the shrine. It is said that teahouses once lined this approach and that it bustled with worshipers. Though this approach lies roughly in the city center, both sides are lined with old pines, and the shrine surroundings are shi-oak woodland with red pine, preserving rich natural environment amid urbanization. Mito Shrine and its pine-lined approach were selected among the "200 Natural Sites of Kyoto."Kyoto Prefecture website, [http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical36.html ''Mito Shrine and the pine-lined approach''] === Access === * 1 km east from {{ill|lt=Joyo Station|Q1049427|WD=Q1049427|1=Jōyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}, north side of {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefectural Joyo High School|Q11375241|WD=Q11375241|1=Jyouyou High School}}. * Immediately at "Joyo Koko" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Keihan Bus|Q11375841|WD=Q11375841|1=Kyoto Keihan Bus}} "Joyo San-san Bus". === Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=373&r=1520450646.0538 Mito Shrine] - {{ill|lt=Kyoto Tourism Federation|Q101072265|WD=Q101072265|1=Kyoto Tourism Federation}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]

The roof is {{ill|lt=hinoki bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} with a large {{ill|lt=chidori hafu|Q4881511|WD=Q4881511|1=Bargeboard}} gable on the front. At the center of the front porch are openwork {{ill|lt=karakusa pattern|Q732328|WD=Q732328|lt=arabesque|1=Scroll (art)}} carvings and {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} braces, making a simple yet elegant structure. A {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|WD=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} indicates construction in {{ill|lt=1448|Q6439|WD=Q6439|1=1448}} ({{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 5) in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. It is the oldest surviving building in Joyo City and is designated a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Mito Jinja Kyoto Honden2.jpg|Honden (side and rear) File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haisho.jpg|Haisho File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), haiden.jpg|Haiden === Festivals === === Annual grand festival === * Every year from {{ill|lt=September 30|Q2881|WD=Q2881|1=September 30}} to {{ill|lt=October 2|Q2932|WD=Q2932|1=October 2}} ** On September 30, the welcoming of the deity called "Oide" is performed in an orderly procession from the otabi site, centered on child mikoshi bearers, with strict order and role assignments for ritual implements. ** On the night of October 1, "Yomiya," a "yudate ritual" is held before the deity at the otabi site, in which a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}} sprinkles hot water from an iron kettle with bamboo on worshipers to pray for safety. ** On October 2, "Matsuri," the deity is escorted back to the shrine in procession. === Okage dance === Mito Shrine preserves the Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property "votive ema of the Okage dance." The ema depicts an Okage dance dedicated on November 1, {{ill|lt=1830|Q7576|WD=Q7576|1=1830}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 13) by people of Kita-higashi-machi, Terada Village. It shows that "Okage dance," said to have often followed group pilgrimages ("Okage-mairi") to Ise Jingu during the Edo period, was also performed here. In Joyo, dances seem to have been performed at festivals in Terada and nearby Aodani until the early Showa period. Today, the local "Okage Dance Preservation Society" has revived and continues the tradition, offering the dance at Mito Shrine.Joyo City Cultural Arts Promotion Plan (March 2007, Joyo City Board of Education Office), [http://www.city.joyo.kyoto.jp/government/plan/plan_culture/download/plan_culture.pdf ''PDF, related pages include pp.25, 27, 50, 51, 54''] === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Mito Shrine honden (with 8 munafuda boards) (building) - built in 1448 (Bun'an 5), mid-Muromachi period. Designated April 14, 1906 (Meiji 39){{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1987|Mito Shrine Honden}}. === Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural properties === * Tangible folk cultural property ** Ema painting of Okage dance - late Edo period work. Registered March 19, 1999 (Heisei 11). === Joyo City designated cultural properties === [[ファイル:Daihannyakyo.jpg|サムネイル|Daihannya Sutra]] * Tangible cultural properties ** Iron kettle - thought to have been used in yudate rituals; inscription shows it was made in {{ill|lt=1425|Q6283|WD=Q6283|1=1425}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 32). ** Complete set of 601 volumes of Daihannya Sutra and 7 sutra chests - sutra copies dating to early {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, important materials for understanding village faith history. Deposited at Joyo City History and Folklore Museum. === Approach === [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), otorii.jpg|thumb|First torii facing old Yamato Kaido, rebuilt September 1966]] [[File:Mito-Jinja (Joyo), torii.jpg|thumb|Second torii at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama; honden is up the stairs ahead]] About 200 m south of JR Joyo Station stands the "first torii" facing the old Yamato Kaido. From there, passing eastward through the "second torii" at the foot of Kōnos巣-yama, the honden is about 600 m ahead. The approach is a gentle uphill slope toward the shrine. It is said that teahouses once lined this approach and that it bustled with worshipers. Though this approach lies roughly in the city center, both sides are lined with old pines, and the shrine surroundings are shi-oak woodland with red pine, preserving rich natural environment amid urbanization. Mito Shrine and its pine-lined approach were selected among the "200 Natural Sites of Kyoto."Kyoto Prefecture website, [http://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/historical36.html ''Mito Shrine and the pine-lined approach''] === Access === * 1 km east from {{ill|lt=Joyo Station|Q1049427|WD=Q1049427|1=Jōyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nara Line|Q1157044|WD=Q1157044|1=Nara Line (JR West)}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}, north side of {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefectural Joyo High School|Q11375241|WD=Q11375241|1=Jyouyou High School}}. * Immediately at "Joyo Koko" bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Kyoto Keihan Bus|Q11375841|WD=Q11375841|1=Kyoto Keihan Bus}} "Joyo San-san Bus". === Notes ===
{{脚注ヘルプ}} === External links === *[http://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=373&r=1520450646.0538 Mito Shrine] - {{ill|lt=Kyoto Tourism Federation|Q101072265|WD=Q101072265|1=Kyoto Tourism Federation}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mito Shrine (Q11548558)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:城陽市の建築物]] [[Category:城陽市の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]
